steps.title: Identify the signal in the but-clause
steps.content: The author introduces demographic characteristics and neighborhood type as factors that “some studies emphasize,” then pivots with “but walking decisions are made in complex contexts in which multiple conditions and needs inform individuals' choices.” This “but” signals the author's purpose: these are real factors identified by research, yet they're only part of a larger picture.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the answer
steps.content:
Choice C matches precisely: these are factors “that have been identified as important influences” (the studies part) but are “merely some of the many factors” (the but-clause's assertion).

The other three mischaracterize the passage—either overstating the scope of these factors' relevance, misrepresenting what people consider, or misinterpreting Alfonzo's argument.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
